mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing activity, which typically leads to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases but SPX forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months, or if the correlation between INDPRO growth and forward returns is negative over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, the market is in a restrictive monetary environment that depresses equity valuations due to higher discount rates, leading to negative forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: If after FEDFUNDS > 5% the average 5-day forward return is positive or zero, or if the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: High housing starts above 1800K units coupled with a rising fed funds rate above 4.5% signal an overheating economy where monetary tightening will slow growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: If the joint condition triggers but SPX forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances, or if the mean return under condition is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is below 4.5%, the labor market is extremely tight, leading to wage inflation pressures and preemptive Fed tightening, which ultimately hurts equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE < 4.5% the 60-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or if the average return is not significantly lower than the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth as measured by real GDP reflects robust economic expansion, which supports corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If GDP growth is positive but SPX 60-day forward returns are negative more often than not (below 50% positive rate).

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 200, inflation is elevated, prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which compresses equity valuations and leads to negative forward returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: If after CPI > 200 the average 20-day forward return is positive or neutral, or if the negative hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production above 110 combined with rising nonfarm payrolls above 135,000 signals strong economic activity that may lead to overheating and eventual Fed tightening, resulting in negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If under the joint condition the mean 20-day forward return is positive, or if the signal does not produce a statistically significant negative average return over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above its 12-month moving average, the monetary policy regime is tightening. In this regime, any further rate increases or sustained high rates lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: If in the tightening regime the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 60% of cases, or if the regime-conditional average return is not significantly lower than the unconditional average.
